desc_breathing: Calm your mind with guided breathing.
right_desc_zero_fir: The Dowry Prohibition Act, passed in 1961, is a law made by the Indian government to stop the practice of dowry in marriages. It makes asking for or accepting dowry a punishable crime. This law is important because it protects women from harassment and unfair treatment and encourages equality and respect in marriage.
right_privacy: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplaces
right_desc_privacy: The Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplaces (Prevention, Prohibition & Redressal) Act, 2013 commonly known as the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Act, 2013 mandates employers to create harassment-free workplaces. By recognizing and reporting harassment, employees can contribute to a more respectful work environment. The Act aims to address complaints of sexual harassment and ensure the safety of employees.
right_desc_sunset: The Right to Equal Pay ensures that women receive the same pay as men for the same work. Supported by various labor laws and international agreements, this principle helps women demand fair pay, reducing gender wage gaps and fostering fairness at work.
right_desc_digital: The Maternity Benefit Act of 1961 guarantees paid leave for women during pregnancy and childbirth. This law acknowledges the importance of supporting women during this critical time and helps them balance work and family responsibilities. Employees are entitled to their full salary during maternity leave, as per the provisions of the Act.
right_desc_domestic: Section 22 (2) of the Factories Act, 1948, mandates safety and health measures for workers in factories. Employers are required to ensure adequate precautions for the health and safety of workers, including provisions for proper ventilation, cleanliness, and sanitation facilities. Understanding this law is crucial for promoting a safe and healthy working environment for all employees.
right_desc_legal_aid: This right ensures that women, especially those with limited resources, can access legal assistance when needed. Understanding this right helps women seek justice without worrying about financial barriers.
right_desc_equal_pay: Section 66 (1) (b) of the Factories Act, 1948, restricts the employment of women in certain occupations during night shifts. This provision aims to protect the safety and well-being of female workers, acknowledging the potential risks associated with nighttime work.
logout_prompt: Are you sure you want to logout?

restart_content: The app needs to restart to apply the language change.

ai_support_desc: SheRise comes with an intelligent AI assistant to help you with safety tips, legal advice, and emotional support. You can access it anytime from the home screen.
clear_chat_warning: This will delete all messages. This action cannot be undone.
error_message: Sorry, I encountered an error. Please try again.
